flow_name: Real QDM — Forward Engineering to SQL
cover_narration: In this walkthrough, we'll forward engineer a data model into runnable SQL using Quest Data Modeler. Start with a model... end with a script you can run on your warehouse. Let's get started.
outro_narration: And there it is. A handful of clicks turns a logical-physical model into ready-to-run T-SQL... primary keys, foreign keys, and indexes included. Thanks for watching.

steps.label: Wait for the Overview page
steps.narration: We start on the Overview page...

steps.label: Open the New Model dialog
steps.narration: First, we need a model to forward engineer. Click New Model...

steps.label: Wait for the New Model dialog
Name the model
steps.narration: Name it... and pick Logical-Physical so the model targets a real database.

steps.label: Click Create
steps.narration: Create the model...

steps.label: Wait for the diagram editor
steps.narration: as the canvas loads.

steps.label: Activate Add Entity
steps.narration: Drop in two quick entities so Forward Engineering has something to generate.

steps.label: Wait for the New Entity dialog
Wait for the second New Entity dialog
Two entities on the canvas
steps.narration: Customer and Orders are now on our canvas.

steps.label: Open the view-mode dropdown
steps.narration: Forward Engineering only operates on the physical side... so switch to the Physical view.

steps.label: Physical view active
steps.narration: Notice the sidebar now shows Tables, Schemas, Stored Procedures... database concepts.

steps.label: Open the Tools menu
steps.narration: Open the Tools menu...

steps.label: Choose Forward Engineering
steps.narration: and choose Forward Engineering.

steps.label: Wait for the FE wizard
steps.narration: The wizard opens. Step one confirms the target database and version.

steps.label: Advance to Option Selection
steps.narration: Click Next...

steps.label: Wait for Option Selection step
steps.narration: Step two lets us tune what gets generated.

steps.label: Advance to Owner Override
steps.narration: We'll keep the defaults and continue.

steps.label: Wait for Owner Override step
steps.narration: Step three handles schema ownership rewrites.

steps.label: Wait for Object Filter step
steps.narration: Step four lets us include or exclude individual tables.

steps.label: Advance to Preview
steps.narration: We want everything... so click Next.

steps.label: Wait for Preview step
steps.narration: And here is the Preview... the generated T-SQL is ready to run on Microsoft Fabric Data Warehouse.

steps.label: Inspect the generated DDL
steps.narration: Every entity becomes a CREATE TABLE statement, with primary keys, indexes, and any relationships translated into foreign-key references.
